Why is /llms.txt coming back as text/plain?
The answer is pretty boring: because the file extension is
Every web server, when it serves a static file, looks up a table called the MIME type map to decide what
header to send. GitHub Pages uses
, which is the industry-standard mapping table. The relevant entries look like this:
— even if you wrote markdown inside it, the web server doesn't peek at the body. It only looks at the extension.
You might be wondering at this point: AI can still read
just fine, right? The content is markdown either way, so what's the big deal?
True — AI can definitely still read it. But there are two small problems:
You lose the explicit format signal:

, it treats the content as plain text and won't necessarily kick in its markdown parsing logic. Headings, lists, links — all that structure can end up being treated as flat prose, and you lose the actual benefit of writing in markdown.
Anthropic and OpenAI both mention in their docs that sites serving markdown should label it as
. That way an AI Agent immediately knows "ah, this is clean markdown intended for me" and treats it accordingly.
So getting Content-Type right matters more than it sounds.
Why a Cloudflare Response Header Transform Rule?

For a need this simple — "I just want to override Content-Type" — the Response Header Transform Rule is the cleanest tool for the job. On top of that, my domain is already on Cloudflare anyway (and gh-pages itself doesn't give you anywhere to touch response headers).
So let me walk through how to set it up.
The whole thing takes maybe a few minutes.

A Cloudflare Response Header Transform Rule fixes
files all at once — and the free plan's 10-rule allowance is more than enough.
When writing the match condition, go with Edit expression mode — it skips the easy mistake of picking "Full URI" instead of "URI Path" in the dropdown.

Once this rule's in place, the next AI crawler that fetches

I dropped /llms.txt onto my gh-pages site, ran curl -I, and found it coming back as text/plain. This post walks through fixing that with a single Cloudflare Response Header Transform Rule — no Worker needed, free plan covers it. Covers why .txt defaults to text/plain via mime-db, the free plan's 10-rule allowance, the full Dashboard setup, how to write the match expression, and verifying with curl -I.
